name: Hot Pepper Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken meat away from the breast bone and slice into squares measuring 1 inch by 1 inch. Dredge the pieces in a mixture of cornstarch and salt on a piece of wax paper. Allow them to dry individually on the wax paper for about 20 minutes.
2. Remove seeds from fresh peppers and coarsely cut into strips. Reserve.
3. Mix brown sugar, cider vinegar, soy sauce, and wine together in a small bowl. Set aside.
4. Heat oil in a wok until smoking. Add the dredged chicken and stir-fry on all sides. If the oil is hot enough, the pieces will not stick to the wok.
5. Add the sugar and vinegar mixture reserved earlier, along with the hot peppers. Place the lid over the wok. Add half a cup of water and cook over medium heat for 5 minutes. The self-gravy should form in the bottom of the wok. Stir the chicken into the gravy so it cooks evenly in the gravy. The gravy should become gelatinous after 5 minutes of cooking. If it becomes too thick, thin it out with small amounts of water.
6. Sprinkle in one cup of peanuts and mix thoroughly.
